steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure question: you must identify how the passage is organized from beginning to end. The decisive clue is the word “But” opening the final sentence, signaling a contrast between the belief stated earlier and what the study actually found.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — “explains how PAs typically affect... then concedes a PA is an exception”: The passage never presents any study supporting the claim that PAs hinder development — the study does the opposite. The passage makes no “exception to a general trend” framing. ✗
  • Choice B — “introduces a widely held belief, proposes a reason for that belief’s prevalence, and then details a study whose findings seemingly conflict with that belief”: Sentence 1 introduces the belief (PAs hinder development). Sentence 2 explains why it persists (assessments miss indirect tourism effects). Sentence 3 (“But”) presents Bhammar et al.’s finding that tourism actually boosts nearby economic sectors — conflicting with the belief. ✓
  • Choice C — “makes a generalization about reconciling economic and conservation priorities”: The passage says nothing about reconciling conservation and economic priorities as a goal — it is about a widespread belief and one study’s contradictory finding. ✗
  • Choice D — “states a hypothesis... outlines findings that support it... then suggests a novel methodology led a separate team to a different conclusion”: Only one research team (Bhammar et al.) is mentioned, not two. Their findings contradict the belief rather than support it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
